generated: Metadata catalog for the collection-of-beauty image archive.

layout.description: One JSON file per source folder under metadata/. Each file contains either a single 'work' object (for the anime screenshot folders) or an 'entries' map keyed by filename (for the Wikimedia-sourced collections).

files.audubon-birds.json: Per-file entries from Wikimedia Commons API (435/435 resolved).
files.kunstformen-images.json: Per-file entries from Wikimedia Commons API (100/100 resolved).
files.collection-of-beauty.json: Per-file entries. 2412/2624 resolved from Wikimedia Commons; the remaining 212 are classified by filename heuristic and marked needs_review where necessary.
files.collection-of-beauty.unresolved.txt: Flat list of the 212 Collection-of-Beauty filenames that the Wikimedia Commons API did not resolve (after thumbnail-prefix stripping, extension-case variants, and NFC normalization).
files.non_wikimedia_sources.json: Reference catalog of non-Wikimedia sources discovered in bookmarks.txt. Use to interpret needs_review entries.
files..cache/: Raw Wikimedia Commons API responses per batch, kept so reruns are free. Safe to delete.

anime_screenshot_files.note: All copyrighted — personal screenshots of Studio Ghibli / CoMix Wave Films / Makoto Shinkai films.

public_domain: All of audubon-birds (John James Audubon, 1785-1851, Birds of America 1827-1838)
All of kunstformen-images (Ernst Haeckel, 1834-1919, Kunstformen der Natur 1904)
The vast majority of collection-of-beauty — Old Master paintings and 19th-century art whose creators died more than 100 years ago
definitely_copyrighted: All anime screenshot folders (6 folders, 1618 files) — Studio Ghibli / CoMix Wave Films
Christie's HGK 2013 auction lots (43 files) — 20th-century Chinese painters
Escher-attributed files (66 files) — M.C. Escher Company B.V., PD 2043
Salvador Dalí works (3 files) — Fundació Gala-Salvador Dalí / VEGAP
Edward Gorey works (5 files) — Edward Gorey Charitable Trust

needs_manual_review.note: Files whose source and copyright status could not be determined even after extended filename heuristics. See collection-of-beauty.unresolved.txt (full 212 list) and filter by needs_review=true in the entries map.
remaining_items: 2× Flickr CDN hashes (40606050395_*, 40606051085_*) — look up via https://www.flickr.com/photo.gne?id=<id>
2× 'Quickenborne' .webp (La gare sous baguettes / Sans Baguettes) — unidentified artist, likely Belgian contemporary
1× Fischer_und_Frau Alexander Zwick.jpg — 'The Fisherman and his Wife' (Brothers Grimm) illustration by Alexander Zwick; not a famous artist, likely a modern book illustration
1× a9d3e89a-...acf.png — bare UUID filename (probably a CMS upload hash, content unknown)
1× H22611-L388076506_original.jpg — auction lot number from Invaluable.com or similar; artist unknown without looking up the lot

1× Jean Metzinger 'Deux Nus' (1910-11) — Cubist work. Metzinger died 1956, so life+70 PMA reaches 2027-01-01. Currently borderline — PD in some jurisdictions, still copyrighted in others. Review when 2027 arrives.

how_to_use.lookup_by_filename: Open metadata/<folder>.json and look up entries[<filename>]. For anime screenshots, any file in that folder shares the single folder-level record.
how_to_use.reproducing_results: All scripts are read-only with respect to the source images. Cache files under metadata/.cache/ allow any rerun to be free (no additional API calls). Delete the cache to force a fresh fetch.
how_to_use.adding_files: Rerun 'node scripts/fetch-wikimedia-metadata.mjs <folder>' then 'node scripts/resolve-unresolved.mjs <folder>' then 'node scripts/classify-unresolved.mjs <folder>' then 'node scripts/normalize-metadata.mjs <folder>.json' (the last step cleans up the multilingual QuickStatement markup and re-scans dates). New files appended to the folder will be picked up automatically.
how_to_use.i18n_translations: Each entry's primary title/description is English. Non-English values live under entry.translations.title[<lang>] and entry.translations.description[<lang>]. Language codes are ISO-639-1 (de, fr, ja, ru, ...) matching Wikidata QS:L<code> labels. Empty translations objects are omitted. Re-run scripts/normalize-metadata.mjs after any fetch to regenerate these.
how_to_use.copyright_review: After normalize-metadata.mjs runs, see metadata/needs_review_dates.txt (entries whose best available year is >= 1926, i.e. possibly still in copyright) and metadata/needs_review_dates.unknown.txt (entries where no creation year could be recovered). Items with an artist_info.died year before 1926 are excluded from the 'unknown' list because the artist was conclusively pre-cutoff.

scripts.scripts/fetch-wikimedia-metadata.mjs: Pass 1: batched Wikimedia Commons API fetch (50 titles/batch, maxlag=5, polite User-Agent).
scripts.scripts/resolve-unresolved.mjs: Pass 2: retry with filename variants (strip 1280px- thumbs, case-swap extensions, NFC normalization) and follow normalization chains.
scripts.scripts/classify-unresolved.mjs: Pass 3: filename-pattern heuristic classification for what the API can't resolve.
scripts.scripts/normalize-metadata.mjs: Pass 4: converts raw QS:P1476/QS:L<code> multilingual strings in title/description into a clean English primary value plus an entry.translations map keyed by language; re-derives entry.year from the most reliable source (Wikidata QS:P571 creation date, Japanese era phrases, filename year, title/description year, 'nth century' phrasing) and writes metadata/needs_review_dates*.txt listing copyright-review candidates.
scripts.scripts/mark-copyright-status.mjs: Pass 5: writes entry.pd_status (public_domain, uraa_restricted, copyrighted, needs_review) and entry.pd_status_reason. URAA_restricted captures shin-hanga artists whose source-country term is shorter than the US term (Hiroshi Yoshida, Hasui Kawase): pre-1931 works stay PD, 1931+ works are flagged. The gallery build in scripts/build-data.mjs drops uraa_restricted entries.
scripts.scripts/artists-db.json: Curated lookup of ~75 prominent artists with birth/death/nationality/movement. Used for enrichment.
